source_docs.title: Genome-wide detection of DNase I hypersensitive sites in single cells and FFPE tissue samples

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq DNA Adapter
oligos.role: Forked (Y-shaped) double-stranded adapter ligated to end-repaired, dA-tailed DHS fragments; carries the Read 1 and Read 2 primer landing sites

components.name: TruSeq Read 1 side strand
components.role: Top adapter strand; provides the Read 1 primer binding sequence

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 side strand
components.role: 5'-phosphorylated bottom adapter strand; provides the Read 2 primer binding sequence

oligos.notes: Sequences not printed in the paper (states library 'prepared using Illumina kits'); canonical TruSeq forked-adapter sequences supplied. The circular carrier DNA is not compatible with adaptor ligation, which suppresses its later amplification.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq P5 amplification primer
oligos.role: Round-1 PCR forward primer (paired with the index primer) that completes the P5/Read1 arm of the library
oligos.notes: First amplification (6 cycles, 98/67/72 C) done with index primers per Methods.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Index PCR Primer (i7)
oligos.role: Round-1 PCR reverse primer that adds the P7 sequence plus the per-cell/per-sample i7 index onto the Read 2 arm

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 primer
components.role: Read 2 primer landing sequence; anneals to the ligated adapter

oligos.notes: Index sequences per single cell/library are not tabulated in the documents; represented as a placeholder. Single indexing (HiSeq 2500, 2015).

oligos.name: Illumina P5 primer
oligos.role: Round-2 PCR forward primer used to bulk-amplify (22 cycles) the small DHS fragments after size selection

oligos.name: Illumina P7 primer
oligos.role: Round-2 PCR reverse primer used to bulk-amplify (22 cycles) the small DHS fragments after size selection

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer
oligos.role: Sequencing primer for Read 1 (reads one end of the genomic DHS insert)

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 primer
oligos.role: Sequencing primer for Read 2 (reads the other end of the genomic DHS insert in paired-end runs)

oligos.name: Illumina Index sequencing primer (i7)
oligos.role: Sequencing primer for the i7 index read

final_library.source_label: scDNase-seq TruSeq single-index genomic DHS library (reconstructed from Jin & Tang et al., Nature 2015; sequences absent from paper filled from canonical Illumina TruSeq)
annotation_lines: TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT = Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer (shares the ACAC overlap with P5)
[CDNA] = genomic DNase I hypersensitive-site insert (open-chromatin fragment)
AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC = Illumina TruSeq Read 2 adapter (reverse complement of Read 2 primer)
[SAMPLE_INDEX:6] = i7 sample index (one index per single-cell library)

evidence.locator: scDNase-seq TruSeq single-index genomic DHS library (reconstructed from Jin & Tang et al., Nature 2015; sequences absent from paper filled from canonical Illumina TruSeq)

library_generation.title: FACS single-cell deposition and lysis
library_generation.summary: A single live cell is sorted into lysis buffer, releasing intact nucleosomal genomic DNA.
library_generation.note: DAPI-stained single live cells are flow-sorted directly into 30 ul cell lysis buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH7.5, 10 mM NaCl, 3 mM MgCl2, 0.1% Triton X-100). For FFPE, cells are scraped from the slide, de-paraffinized and lysed instead.
library_generation.product:
5'- XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX -3'
3'- XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX -5'
(genomic DNA packaged in nucleosomes; open regions = DHSs)

library_generation.title: DNase I digestion of open chromatin
library_generation.summary: 0.2-1 U DNase I preferentially cleaves accessible (hypersensitive) chromatin into short double-stranded fragments.
library_generation.note: Roche DNase I (04716728001), 37 C for 5 min. Cutting is biased to DHSs, generating heterogeneous small dsDNA fragments (<200 bp) from open chromatin.
library_generation.product:
5'- XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX -3'
3'- XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX -5'
(<200 bp DHS-derived fragments with ragged ends)

library_generation.title: Stop reaction, add circular carrier DNA, purify
library_generation.summary: Digestion is stopped and ~30 ng of circular plasmid carrier DNA is spiked in before phenol-chloroform/ethanol purification.
library_generation.note: Stop buffer contains SDS/EDTA + proteinase K; 5 ul of 6 ng/ul circular carrier DNA (~3x10^5 fold over single-cell DHS DNA) prevents loss of the <0.1 pg of released DNA. The circular carrier cannot be ligated to adapters, limiting its downstream amplification. For FFPE, crosslinks are reversed at 65 C overnight.
library_generation.product:
5'- XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX -3'      +   (((  circular carrier plasmid  )))
3'- XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX -5'          (supercoiled; NOT ligation-competent)

library_generation.title: End-repair and dA-tailing
library_generation.summary: Fragment ends are blunted and 3' A-tailed to prepare for T-overhang TruSeq adapter ligation.
library_generation.note: Standard Illumina TruSeq end-repair converts ragged ends to blunt, phosphorylated ends; Klenow exo- adds a single 3' dA overhang.
library_generation.product:
5'-   XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX A -3'
3'- A XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX   -5'
(blunt, 5'-phosphorylated, single 3' dA overhangs)

library_generation.title: TruSeq forked-adapter ligation
library_generation.summary: Y-shaped TruSeq adapters are ligated to both ends of each DHS fragment; circular carrier remains unligated.
library_generation.note: The forked adapter creates asymmetric ends carrying the Read 1 and Read 2 primer landing sites, so only ligated fragments become PCR-amplifiable.
library_generation.product:
5'- ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT XXXX...XXXX AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA -3'
3'-           GAGTGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA XXXX...XXXX TCTAGCCTTCTCACACGTGTGCA           -5'
(forked/Y adapters on both ends; carrier plasmid not ligated)

library_generation.title: First PCR with index primers (6 cycles)
library_generation.summary: The P5 amplification primer and the indexed P7 primer add full P5/P7 ends and the sample index, preferentially amplifying the small DHS fragments.
library_generation.note: 98 C 10 s / 67 C 30 s / 72 C 30 s x6. Short cycle count plus the non-ligatable carrier minimizes non-specific carrier amplification.

library_generation.title: Size selection of DHS fragments (160-300 bp)
library_generation.summary: The desired small-insert library molecules (160-300 bp) are isolated on a 2% E-gel, removing carrier and large products.
library_generation.note: Enriches for the short fragments derived from DNase I hypersensitive sites.

library_generation.title: Second PCR with P5 and P7 primers (22 cycles)
library_generation.summary: Universal short P5 and P7 primers bulk-amplify the size-selected library.
library_generation.note: 98 C 10 s / 68 C 30 s / 72 C 30 s x22; primes off the P5/P7 sequences already added in round 1 to generate sequenceable amounts.

library_generation.title: Final size selection and sequencing
library_generation.summary: Fragments between 160 and 300 bp are re-isolated on E-gel and sequenced on Illumina HiSeq 2500.
library_generation.note: Yields the final sequenceable TruSeq single-index genomic DHS library.

library_sequencing.note: Reads one end of the genomic DHS insert (top-strand sequence). Most single-cell libraries were single-end; some were paired-end (paired reads with insert distance <500 bp were retained). The Read 1 primer is identical to a top-strand segment, so it anneals to the bottom strand and is extended into the insert.

Reads the 6-nt i7 sample index identifying the single-cell/sample library. The index primer is identical to the top-strand Read 2 adapter segment, so it anneals to the bottom strand and is extended rightward into the index.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index primer  5'-GATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC-3'
                                                     ------------------------------------->  (read = NNNNNN i7 index)
            5'- ...AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC NNNNNN ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
            3'- ...TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG NNNNNN TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'   (template = bottom)

library_sequencing.note: In paired-end runs, reads the opposite end of the genomic DHS insert (bottom-strand sequence). The Read 2 primer is the bottom-strand adapter sequence, so it anneals to the top strand and is extended leftward into the insert.

title: scDNase-seq
description: scDNase-seq (also called Pico-Seq) is an ultrasensitive DNase I hypersensitive site (DHS) sequencing method that maps genome-wide chromatin accessibility in single cells or in tiny cell pools recovered from FFPE tissue. FACS-sorted single cells are lysed and lightly digested with DNase I, which cuts preferentially at open (hypersensitive) chromatin; because a single cell releases <0.1 pg of DHS DNA, a large excess of circular carrier plasmid DNA (~30 ng) is added before a standard Illumina TruSeq end-repair/adaptor-ligation library prep. A two-step PCR (index primers, then P5/P7 primers) preferentially amplifies the small DHS-derived fragments over the non-ligatable circular carrier. The short reads simply map genomic fragments derived from accessible chromatin; there is no cell barcode or UMI because each single cell is processed one-per-tube and carries one sample index.

reference.label: Genome-wide detection of DNase I hypersensitive sites in single cells and FFPE tissue samples

original_publication.title: Genome-wide detection of DNase I hypersensitive sites in single cells and FFPE tissue samples

throughput.summary: Detects genome-wide DHSs from single cells up to ~20 million cells; on average ~317,000 unique reads and ~38,000 DHSs per single cell, with 23-26% of reads falling in DHS regions.
throughput.cells: Single cell up to ~20 million cells (single, 100, 1,000, 10,000-cell and bulk libraries; also FFPE tumour/normal cell pools)
throughput.dna: <0.1 pg of DNase I-released DHS DNA per single cell, rescued with ~30 ng circular carrier plasmid DNA (~3x10^5-fold excess)

publication.statistical_model: DHSs called with MACS (Poisson-based peak model); differential (tumour/normal, cell-type-specific) DHSs identified with edgeR (negative binomial); cell-specificity assessed by Wilcoxon signed-rank and Fisher's exact tests; a custom noise/FDR model estimates false-positive DHSs from genome-wide background read density.

other.label: Carrier DNA
other.value: ~30 ng circular plasmid DNA per reaction (~3x10^5-fold over single-cell DHS DNA); not ligation-competent, suppressing carrier amplification

other.label: Two-step PCR
other.value: 6 cycles with index primers, size-select 160-300 bp, then 22 cycles with P5/P7 primers

other.label: Single-cell mappability
other.value: ~2% (vs ~40% for 1,000-cell libraries) owing to non-specific carrier-DNA amplification

other.label: Reference genomes / aligner
other.value: mouse mm9 / human hg18, mapped with Bowtie2

other.label: Clinical finding
other.value: Detected a de novo SNV chr18:52417839 G>C near TXNL1 in follicular thyroid carcinoma cells that disrupts a p53 binding motif and correlates with decreased TXNL1 expression

other.label: Comparison to scATAC-seq
other.value: scDNase-seq recovers many more DHSs per cell than Tn5-based scATAC-seq, enabling cell-to-cell variation analysis at individual regulatory regions

data_processing.summary: Reads are mapped to mm9/hg18 with Bowtie2 using iterative 5-bp trimming re-alignment, low-quality and redundant reads are removed, and DHS peaks are called with MACS; tag densities are normalized per bin and compared across libraries by Pearson correlation, while differential/cell-specific DHSs, tumour SNVs, super-enhancers and GO enrichment are derived with edgeR, SAMtools, ROSE and GREAT/DAVID/GSEA. A custom genome-wide background-noise model estimates the false-positive (FDR) rate of single-cell DHSs.
data_processing.statistical_model: Poisson-based peak model (MACS) for DHS calling; negative binomial (edgeR) for differential DHSs; custom genome-wide background-noise model for single-cell DHS FDR
